name: rcss3d_agent_msgs
description: Custom messages for communicating with rcss3d_agent

Accelerometer.description: The proper acceleration it experiences relative to free fall. As a consequence an accelerometer
Accelerometer.raw:
# The proper acceleration it experiences relative to free fall. As a consequence an accelerometer
# at rest relative to the Earth's surface will indicate approximately 1g (9.81m/s^2) upwards. To
# obtain the acceleration due to motion with respect to the earth, this gravity offset should be
# subtracted.

string name  # The name of the body containing the accelerometer
float32 x  # Acceleration along the x-axis in m/s2
float32 y  # Acceleration along the y-axis in m/s2
float32 z  # Acceleration along the z-axis in m/s2

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the body containing the accelerometer

fields.name: x
fields.comment: Acceleration along the x-axis in m/s2

fields.name: y
fields.comment: Acceleration along the y-axis in m/s2

fields.name: z
fields.comment: Acceleration along the z-axis in m/s2

AgentState.description: Information about the internal state of the agent. It reports information about the current
AgentState.raw:
# Information about the internal state of the agent. It reports information about the current
# battery status and the temperature of the agent.

float32 temp  # Degrees Celsius
float32 battery  # Percentile

fields.name: temp
fields.comment: Degrees Celsius

fields.name: battery
fields.comment: Percentile

Beam.description: The beam effector allows a player to position itself on the field before the start of each half.
Beam.raw:
# The beam effector allows a player to position itself on the field before the start of each half.
# The x and y coordinates define the position on the field with respect to the field's coordinate
# system, where (0,0) is the absolute center of the field. The rot specifies the facing angle of
# the player. Zero points to positive x-axis, 90 to positive y-axis.

float64 x  # metres
float64 y  # metres
float64 rot  # degrees

fields.name: x
fields.comment: metres

fields.name: y
fields.comment: metres

fields.name: rot
fields.comment: degrees

Effector.description: Effectors allow agents to perform actions within the simulation. Agents control them by
Effector.raw:
# Effectors allow agents to perform actions within the simulation. Agents control them by
# sending messages to the server, and the server changes the game state accordingly.
# Effectors are the logical dual of perceptors.
# Details of each message type are explained in each msg file.

HingeJointVel[] hinge_joint_vels
UniversalJointVel[] universal_joint_vels
Beam[] beams
Say[] says

Flag.raw:
string name  # F1L, F1R, F2L or F2R
Spherical base  # The point at the base of the flag's pole

fields.name: name
fields.comment: F1L, F1R, F2L or F2R

fields.name: base
fields.comment: The point at the base of the flag's pole

ForceResistance.description: The force that acts on a body. [px, py, pz] describes the point of origin relative to the body
ForceResistance.raw:
# The force that acts on a body. [px, py, pz] describes the point of origin relative to the body
# itself and [fx, fy, fz] describes the resulting force on this point. The two vectors are just
# an approximation about the real applied force. The point of origin is calculated as weighted
# average of all contact points to which the force is applied, while the force vector represents
# the total force applied to all of these contact points. The information to a force resistance
# perceptor is just sent in case of a present collision of the corresponding body with another
# simulation object. If there is no force applied, the message of this perceptor is omitted.

string name  # The name of the body, to which the force resistance perceptor belongs
float32 px  # The local x coordinate of the origin of the applied force in meters
float32 py  # The local y coordinate of the origin of the applied force in meters
float32 pz  # The local z coordinate of the origin of the applied force in meters
float32 fx  # The x component of the force vector in newton (kg m/s2)
float32 fy  # The y component of the force vector in newton (kg m/s2)
float32 fz  # The z component of the force vector in newton (kg m/s2)

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the body, to which the force resistance perceptor belongs

fields.name: px
fields.comment: The local x coordinate of the origin of the applied force in meters

fields.name: py
fields.comment: The local y coordinate of the origin of the applied force in meters

fields.name: pz
fields.comment: The local z coordinate of the origin of the applied force in meters

fields.name: fx
fields.comment: The x component of the force vector in newton (kg m/s2)

fields.name: fy
fields.comment: The y component of the force vector in newton (kg m/s2)

fields.name: fz
fields.comment: The z component of the force vector in newton (kg m/s2)

GameState.description: Contains several information about the actual state of the soccer game environment. The actual
GameState.raw:
# Contains several information about the actual state of the soccer game environment. The actual
# play time and play mode are transmitted in each cycle. Play time starts from zero at kickoff and
# is given as a floating point number in seconds, to two decimal places.

float32 time  # The current play time in seconds
string playmode  # The current play mode of the soccer game (eg. BeforeKickOff)
int32 score_left  # The score of the left team
int32 score_right  # The score of the right team

fields.name: time
fields.comment: The current play time in seconds

fields.name: playmode
fields.comment: The current play mode of the soccer game (eg. BeforeKickOff)

fields.name: score_left
fields.comment: The score of the left team

fields.name: score_right
fields.comment: The score of the right team

Goalpost.raw:
string name  # G1L, G1R, G2L or G2R
Spherical top  # The point at the top of the goal's post

fields.name: name
fields.comment: G1L, G1R, G2L or G2R

fields.name: top
fields.comment: The point at the top of the goal's post

GyroRate.description: Information about the change in orientation of a body. The message contains the name of the body
GyroRate.raw:
# Information about the change in orientation of a body. The message contains the name of the body
# to which the gyro perceptor belongs and three rotation angles. These rotation angles describe the
# change rates in orientation of the body during the last cycle. In other words the current angular
# velocities along the three axes of freedom of the corresponding body in degrees per second.
# To keep track of the orientation of the body, the information to each gyro rate perceptor is sent
# every cycle.

string name  # The name of the body part the gyro rate perceptor is attached to
float32 x  # The current angular velocities along the x axis in degrees per second
float32 y  # The current angular velocities along the y axis in degrees per second
float32 z  # The current angular velocities along the z axis in degrees per second

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the body part the gyro rate perceptor is attached to

fields.name: x
fields.comment: The current angular velocities along the x axis in degrees per second

fields.name: y
fields.comment: The current angular velocities along the y axis in degrees per second

fields.name: z
fields.comment: The current angular velocities along the z axis in degrees per second

Hear.description: Agent processes are not allowed to communicate with each other directly, but agents may exchange
Hear.raw:
# Agent processes are not allowed to communicate with each other directly, but agents may exchange
# messages via the simulation server. For this purpose agents are equipped with the so-called hear
# perceptor, which serves as an aural sensor and receives messages shouted by other players.
#
# See restrictions: https://gitlab.com/robocup-sim/SimSpark/-/wikis/Perceptors#restrictions

string team  # The team of the player who sent the message
float32 time  # The simulation time at which the given message was heard in seconds.
bool self  # Whether the player is hearing their own words.
float32[<=1] direction  # Direction of heard message, if the message wasn't the player's own words.
                        # Field has zero size if the "self" field is true.
string message  # Up to 20 characters, which may be taken from the ASCII printing character subset
                # [0x20, 0x7E] except the white space character and the normal brackets ( and )

fields.name: team
fields.comment: The team of the player who sent the message

fields.name: time
fields.comment: The simulation time at which the given message was heard in seconds.

fields.name: self
fields.comment: Whether the player is hearing their own words.

fields.name: direction
fields.comment: Direction of heard message, if the message wasn't the player's own words.

fields.name: message
fields.comment: Up to 20 characters, which may be taken from the ASCII printing character subset

HingeJointPos.description: Information about the angle of the correponding single-axis hinge joint. A zero angle
HingeJointPos.raw:
# Information about the angle of the correponding single-axis hinge joint. A zero angle
# corresponds to straightly aligned bodies. The position angle of each hinge joint
# perceptor is sent every cycle.
#
# Each hinge joint has minimum and maximum limits on its angular position. This varies
# from hinge to hinge and depends upon the model being used.

string name  # The name of the corresponding hinge joint
float32 ax  # The current position angle in degrees

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the corresponding hinge joint

fields.name: ax
fields.comment: The current position angle in degrees

HingeJointVel.description: Effector for all axis with a single degree of freedom.
HingeJointVel.raw:
# Effector for all axis with a single degree of freedom.
# name - name of the axis.
# ax - a speed value, passed in radians per second.
# Setting a speed value on a hinge means that the speed will be maintained until a new value is provided. 
# Even if the hinge meets its extremity, it will bounce around at the extremity until a new
# speed value is requested.

string name  # The name of the corresponding hinge joint
float32 ax  # The angular velocity in radians per second

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the corresponding hinge joint

fields.name: ax
fields.comment: The angular velocity in radians per second

Percept.description: Perceptors are the senses of an agent, allowing awareness of the agent's model state and the
Percept.raw:
# Perceptors are the senses of an agent, allowing awareness of the agent's model state and the
# environment. The server sends perceptor messages to agents, via the network protocol, for every
# cycle of the simulation. Details of each message type are explained in each msg file.

GyroRate[] gyro_rates
HingeJointPos[] hinge_joints
UniversalJointPos[] universal_joints
ForceResistance[] force_resistances
Accelerometer[] accelerometers
Vision[<=1] vision
GameState game_state
AgentState[<=1] agent_state
Hear[] hears

Player.description: An observed player is described through the name of it's team, the player number and one or more
Player.raw:
# An observed player is described through the name of it's team, the player number and one or more
# position vectors to different body parts.

string team  # The name of the team, to which the seen player belongs.
int32 id  # The player number of the seen player
Spherical[<=1] head  # Observation of head
Spherical[<=1] rlowerarm  # Observation of right lower arm
Spherical[<=1] llowerarm  # Observation of left lower arm
Spherical[<=1] rfoot  # Observation of right foot
Spherical[<=1] lfoot  # Observation of left foot

fields.name: team
fields.comment: The name of the team, to which the seen player belongs.

fields.name: id
fields.comment: The player number of the seen player

fields.name: head
fields.comment: Observation of head

fields.name: rlowerarm
fields.comment: Observation of right lower arm

fields.name: llowerarm
fields.comment: Observation of left lower arm

fields.name: rfoot
fields.comment: Observation of right foot

fields.name: lfoot
fields.comment: Observation of left foot

Say.description: The say effector permits communication among agents by broadcasting messages. In order to say
Say.raw:
# The say effector permits communication among agents by broadcasting messages. In order to say 
# something, the following command has to be employed.
#
# See restrictions: https://gitlab.com/robocup-sim/SimSpark/-/wikis/Perceptors#restrictions

string message  # Up to 20 characters, which may be taken from the ASCII printing character subset
                # [0x20, 0x7E] except the white space character and the normal brackets ( and )

fields.name: message
fields.comment: Up to 20 characters, which may be taken from the ASCII printing character subset

Spherical.description: Spherical msg used for soccer vision perceptors. All distances and angles are given
Spherical.raw:
# Spherical msg used for soccer vision perceptors. All distances and angles are given
# relative to the position and orientation of the camera.
#
# Details: https://gitlab.com/robocup-sim/SimSpark/-/wikis/Perceptors#vision-perceptors
#
# Angles are stored in degrees.

# The distance to the seen object, in metres.
float32 r

# The horizontal angle (φ) to the visible object relative to the view direction in degrees
# and an accuracy of two digits. Positive angles are left of the vertical midline of the
# agent's vision, negative angles right.
float32 phi

# The vertical angle (θ) to the visible object relative to the view direction in degrees
# and an accuracy of two digits. Positive angles mean the seen object is above the
# horizontal midline of agent's vision, negative means below.
float32 theta

Synchronize.description: Synchronize Effector - Agents running in Agent Sync Mode must send this command at the end of
Synchronize.raw:
# Synchronize Effector - Agents running in Agent Sync Mode must send this command at the end of
# each simulation cycle. 
# The server ignores this command if it is received in Real-Time Mode, so it is safe to 
# configure your agent to always append this command to your agent's responses.

UniversalJointPos.description: Information about the two angles of the correponding two axis universal joint. Zero degrees
UniversalJointPos.raw:
# Information about the two angles of the correponding two axis universal joint. Zero degrees
# corresponds to straightly aligned bodies.

string name  # The name of the corresponding universal joint
float32 ax1  # Current position angle of the first axis in degrees
float32 ax2  # Current position angle of the second axis in degrees

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the corresponding universal joint

fields.name: ax1
fields.comment: Current position angle of the first axis in degrees

fields.name: ax2
fields.comment: Current position angle of the second axis in degrees

UniversalJointVel.description: Effector for all axis with a two degrees of freedom. The first parameter is the name of the axis.
UniversalJointVel.raw:
# Effector for all axis with a two degrees of freedom. The first parameter is the name of the axis.
# The second and third parameters contain the change in angle of the two joints. The order of the
# joints is the same as in the name.

string name  # The name of the corresponding hinge joint
float32 ax1  # The angular velocity of the first axis in radians per second
float32 ax2  # The angular velocity of the second axis in radians per second

fields.name: name
fields.comment: The name of the corresponding hinge joint

fields.name: ax1
fields.comment: The angular velocity of the first axis in radians per second

fields.name: ax2
fields.comment: The angular velocity of the second axis in radians per second

Vision.description: Message consisting of observed vision information.
Vision.raw:
# Message consisting of observed vision information. 
# Details: https://gitlab.com/robocup-sim/SimSpark/-/wikis/Perceptors#vision-perceptors

Ball[<=1] ball
FieldLine[] field_lines
Flag[] flags
Goalpost[] goalposts
Player[] players
